title: PG-002: Migration runner with dual SQLite + Postgres CI support
consumer_purpose: Enable developers to run Drizzle schema migrations against either SQLite or Postgres from a single command, with CI verifying both paths
claim_atoms: Migration runner script exists at src/storage/migrate.ts with programmatic API and CLI entry point
Drizzle PG config exists at drizzle.config.pg.ts for generating PG migrations
package.json has migrate, migrate:pg, db:generate, db:generate:pg scripts
CI has a pg job that runs Postgres tests with a real PG service container
Migration runner test exists at test/storage/migrate.test.ts that exercises both dialects
PG migration test exists at test/storage/migration.pg.test.ts that verifies applyMigrations works
content:
## PG-002: Migration Runner with Dual Dialect Support

### Root Causes

Four independent gaps prevent developers from running Drizzle migrations against either dialect from a single command:
1. Gap A: applyMigrations() in src/storage/db.pg.ts is defined and exported but unreachable from any startup path — dead code with zero call sites.
2. Gap B: No Drizzle Kit Postgres config is committed — the only drizzle.config.ts uses dialect: "sqlite".
3. Gap C: No npm scripts exist for running or generating migrations — only a bare db script.
4. Gap D: CI has no Postgres job — test:pg script exists but is never invoked in test.yml.

### Design Decisions

#### 1. Dialect Detection (Priority Order)
1. CLI flag --pg or --sqlite (highest)
2. OPENCODE_DATABASE_DIALECT=sqlite|pg — explicit override env var
3. OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL is set → PG
4. Otherwise → SQLite

#### 2. Migration Runner Architecture (src/storage/migrate.ts)

SQLite path:
- Opens bun:sqlite Database at the configured path
- Applies migration/ folder via drizzle-orm/bun-sqlite/migrator
- DB path resolved: --db-path > OPENCODE_SQLITE_DB_PATH > Global.Path.data + "/opencode.db"
- Does NOT import from db.ts to avoid Effect runtime coupling
- try/finally ensures sqlite.close()

PG path:
- Uses init() from #db to create client (PGlite for :memory:/file paths, node-postgres for connection strings)
- Applies migration-pg/ via applyMigrations() from db.pg.ts
- Connection string from OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL or --conn-string CLI arg
- Guard: throws clear error if neither source provides a URL
- try/finally ensures underlying client pool is closed via (client as any).$client.end()

#### 3. Programmatic API
```ts
export async function runMigrations(opts?: MigrateOptions): Promise<void>
export type Dialect = "sqlite" | "pg"
export interface MigrateOptions {
  dialect?: Dialect
  dbPath?: string
  connectionString?: string
  ssl?: boolean
}
```

#### 4. Drizzle PG Config (drizzle.config.pg.ts)
- dialect: "postgresql"
- schema: "./src/**/*.pg.sql.ts"
- out: "./migration-pg"

#### 5. npm Scripts
- migrate → bun run src/storage/migrate.ts
- migrate:pg → OPENCODE_DATABASE_DIALECT=pg bun run src/storage/migrate.ts
- db:generate → node ./node_modules/drizzle-kit/bin.cjs generate
- db:generate:pg → node ./node_modules/drizzle-kit/bin.cjs generate --config=drizzle.config.pg.ts

#### 6. CI Postgres Job
New pg job in test.yml with Postgres 16 service container, linux-only, runs targeted PG tests with OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL set.

#### 7. Resource Cleanup
PG path wraps initPg + applyMigrations in try/finally and calls (client as any).$client.end() if the underlying client has an end() function (node-postgres Pool). PGlite clients (in-memory) have no end() — skipped via duck-type check.

## Files To Create

### File 1: packages/opencode/src/storage/migrate.ts
Full implementation with:
- detectDialect(), resolveDbPath(), runMigrations() exports
- CLI entry point via import.meta.main
- try/finally resource cleanup in both SQLite and PG paths
- No dead code (MIGRATIONS_PG removed since applyMigrations hardcodes its own path)

### File 2: packages/opencode/drizzle.config.pg.ts
Simple drizzle-kit config for postgresql dialect.

### File 3: packages/opencode/test/storage/migrate.test.ts
Tests runner API with both dialects (in-memory), auto-detection, flags, env vars, error paths.

### File 4: packages/opencode/test/storage/migration.pg.test.ts
Tests PG migrations end-to-end with PGlite. Uses @/storage/db.pg import alias for consistency.

## Files To Modify

### File 5: packages/opencode/package.json
Add 4 new scripts (migrate, migrate:pg, db:generate, db:generate:pg) — additive, no existing script names changed.

### File 6: .github/workflows/test.yml
Add pg job after e2e job with Postgres 16 service container, targeted PG test run, JUnit reporting.

## Impact Analysis & Risks

| Change | Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| New migrate.ts imports Global from @opencode-ai/core/global | Low — Global is a simple path resolver | Verified by existing usage |
| Reusing applyMigrations from db.pg.ts | Low — path resolution identical from migrate.ts and db.pg.ts | Verified |
| New npm scripts (additive) | None — 4 new scripts with no overlap | N/A |
| CI pg job | Low — new job does not affect existing jobs | Only runs on linux |
| Drizzle PG config references .pg.sql.ts bridge files | Low — trivial re-exports | Verified |
| PG client resource leak | Fixed — try/finally calls .end() on underlying pool | Duck-type check on $client.end() |

## Edge Cases
1. Empty migration folders — no-op, no error
2. Migration failure mid-way — __drizzle_migrations tracks completed; re-runnable
3. Missing URL for PG — clear error: "OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL is required for PG migrations"
4. In-memory SQLite — works for testing
5. PGlite with :memory: — already tested in adapter.pg.test.ts
6. PG pool cleanup on error — try/finally ensures end() is called even if applyMigrations throws

## Validation Strategy
1. bun run typecheck in packages/opencode
2. bun run src/storage/migrate.ts --sqlite --db-path :memory:
3. bun run src/storage/migrate.ts --pg --conn-string :memory:
4. bun test test/storage/migrate.test.ts
5. bun test test/storage/migration.pg.test.ts
6. bun test --timeout 30000 (full suite regression)
revision_reason: Fix PG client resource leak (blocking finding from critic review): add try/finally cleanup for PG client pool. Remove dead MIGRATIONS_PG constant. Use @/storage/db.pg alias for test import consistency.
